channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: From Chuck To AOC, Here Are The Worst People Of The Week
item.description: From Chuck Schumer’s race-baiting false equivalencies to Lisa Murkowski being, well, Lisa Murkowski, it was a great week for bad people.

item.title: Candles and tablecloth at White Castle: How a Valentine’s Day tradition sprouted over 30 years ago and spread nationwide
item.description: The "fun and quirky" promotion started in 1991 and was only offered at White Castles in St. Louis and Minneapolis.

item.title: Collins boosts Republican voter ID effort, but won’t scrap filibuster
item.description: While Sen. Susan Collins, R-Maine, supports the SAVE America Act, she won't buck the Senate filibuster, which will prove a key barrier in the legislat...

item.title: The Ugly Deportation Case of Rumeysa Öztürk
item.description: The administration may appeal the latest decision and might well win. But its conduct has conveyed weakness in several respects.

item.title: Trump trounces Biden energy records in just months as admin celebrates 1 year of 'historic gains': data
item.description: White House marks National Energy Dominance Council anniversary with Interior Secretary Doug Burgum touting energy production gains and lower gas pric...

item.title: New York’s Museum of Sex: Serious, Sly, Fun, and Funky
item.description: A collection on sex through the ages explores its power, delights — oh, and there’s plenty of paraphernalia.

item.title: Victorian-era ‘vinegar valentines’ show that trolling existed long before social media or the internet
item.description: In 1885, a resident of Birmingham, UK, was charged with the attempted murder of his estranged wife after he received a vinegar valentine from her.

item.title: Trump Should End the FDA’s 25-Year Cover-Up on the Abortion Pill
item.description: Mifepristone has not gotten safer; the FDA has just gotten more political.

item.title: A U.S. ‘debt spiral’ could start in coming years when the interest rate on government borrowing exceeds economic growth, budget watchdog says
item.description: CRFB warned the actual fiscal outlook could be far worse than even the latest sobering projections.

item.title: Conservative firebrand launches ‘TruckSafe Tipline’ to report illegal drivers amid spike in highway deaths
item.description: Amid heightened concern over highway deaths involving illegal drivers, Sen. Jim Banks, R-Ind., is launching a “TruckSafe Tipline" for truckers to shar...

item.title: The $31 million question: Are Trump's settlements actually changing journalism?
item.description: Two major media outlets have paid President Trump tens of millions to settle defamation lawsuits, but the expensive capitulations have produced only m...

item.title: Americans wake up and smell the coffee price surge—skipping Starbucks, brewing at home, and drinking Diet Coke for caffeine
item.description: Coffee prices in the U.S. were up 18.3% in January from a year ago. Over five years, coffee prices rose 47%.

item.title: U.S., Iran to hold new round of nuclear talks in Geneva this week, Swiss government says
item.description: Iran and the United States will hold a second round of talks over Tehran's nuclear program next week, the Swiss Foreign Ministry said Saturday.

item.title: Iran's crown prince says survival of Tehran government 'sends a clear signal to every bully'
item.description: Some 200,000 people demonstrated Saturday against Iran's government on the sidelines of a gathering of world leaders in Germany, police said, answerin...

item.title: Gallup’s Presidential Approval Misstep
item.description: The polling industry seems increasingly uninterested in its own history.

item.title: ICE officers face criminal probe for alleged 'untruthful statements' under oath about Minneapolis shooting
item.description: Two ICE officers face a criminal investigation after allegedly making "untruthful statement" under oath about a Minneapolis shooting involving Venezue...

item.title: Gen Z’s latest revolt over Jeffrey Epstein: pointing out a connection to the company that takes class photos
item.description: Some school districts are cancelling school photos as the company, Lifetouch, called the claims “completely false.”

item.title: Fetterman slams Democrats' 'Jim Crow 2.0' voter ID rhetoric as party unity fractures
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman, D-Pa., breaks with Senate Democrats on voter ID legislation, rejecting the "Jim Crow 2.0" characterization of the SAVE Act.

item.title: Russia killed opposition leader with frog poison, 5 European countries say
item.description: Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny was "highly likely" killed in 2024 by a toxin found in poison dart frogs, five European nations said in a sta...

item.title: Stephen A. Smith teases 2028 presidential run
item.description: Sports commentator Stephen A. Smith said he is not ruling out a 2028 presidential campaign.

item.title: AI and Our Collapsing Creative Horizons
item.description: The real danger AI poses is to original thought itself.

item.title: Virginia High Court Greenlights Democrat Effort To Give Millions Of Republicans A Single Seat In Congress
item.description: The Virginia Supreme Court handed Democrats a massive win Friday, allowing them to move forward with a redistricting referendum that would gerrymander...

item.title: The Nixon Library Is Wrong About Nixon And The Deep State
item.description: The new revelation about Nixon 'bears directly on allegations by President Trump and his supporters about the existence of what was once called the pe...

item.title: Here Are 10 Great Justice Scalia Quotes To Mark A Decade Since His Passing
item.description: Here are some of Justice Scalia's best moments in remembrance of the 10-year anniversary of his passing.

item.title: Turns Out The Irishman Detained By ICE Was Facing Drug Charges Back Home
item.description: The arrest of Irishman Seamus Culleton fueled a false media narrative that ICE is grabbing law-abiding people who have done nothing wrong.

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
